title: inula helenium
intro: Inula helenium, also known as Elecampane, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall stems and yellow, daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Inula helenium include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and reduce fever. Inula helenium can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried root, which can be used to make capsules or added to salves and creams for topical application.
